description: Order-preserving wrapper migration options. Plans are mutually exclusive when hook ids overlap.

risk_notes: statusMessage cannot be represented one-for-one in a single settings hook

status_message_policy.reason: Claude settings exposes one statusMessage per hook entry. This wrapper preserves the only existing child statusMessage at wrapper level while child stdout/stderr and per-hook timeouts remain preserved by the runner.

execution_contract.reason: Stop hooks are fail-open background/finalization work. The composite router must continue later Stop phases even if one child exits nonzero, while preserving child stdout/stderr for user-visible warnings.

status_message_policy.reason: The composite Stop router has multiple visible output channels but Claude settings exposes one statusMessage per hook entry. A wrapper-level message makes the whole Stop pipeline visible while child stdout/stderr/audio/notification behavior is preserved by the runner and output contract.

execution_contract.reason: PreToolUse gates can block the original tool call. A wrapper must return the first blocking child exit code and must not run later child gates after a blocking decision.

execution_contract.reason: PreToolUse commit hooks can block the original git commit. A wrapper must return the first nonzero child exit code and must not run later child hooks after a blocking decision.
